channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: China expanding its industrial dominance, warns US business group — 18 replies — 72.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
China expanding its industrial dominance, warns US business group

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-05-16 17:53 UTC

OP

>The US Chamber of Commerce has warned that countries have only a “finite” window to respond to Chinese policies that are deepening reliance on its supply chains and harming the global economy.
>The Washington-based lobby group said Beijing was “doubling down” on state intervention in manufacturing, services and frontier technologies.
>It said China was ushering in a “new phase of global impact” marked by rising trade dependence and a rapid global expansion by its companies. It is also using tools such as export controls to entrench its position in global supply chains and counter foreign diversification strategies.
>The warning came in the preface to a report on new Chinese industrial policies produced for the chamber by Rhodium Group, a consultancy. The chamber said the world had underestimated previous Chinese policies, including the Made in China 2025 programme to make the country more self-reliant in critical technologies.
>“The challenge the world now faces is not the result of an intelligence gap...Reports were published. The warnings reached senior levels of government and industry across major economies. Yet in too many cases, the response was insufficient,” the chamber concluded.
>Rhodium said China’s industrial policy was evolving from sectoral intervention to an “industrial policy of everything”. It said Beijing wanted to extend its dominance in industries such as critical minerals and magnets to a broader range of industrial products. Beijing was also putting more attention on services, it added.
https://archive.ph/snjef

Early replies (first 3)

62238059
>The report said sustained government support and weak domestic demand had driven a rapid expansion of its goods trade surplus — doubling to $2tn since 2019 — in what some have dubbed “China Shock 2.0” as the country rapidly moves away from an economy based on low-cost manufacturing.
>It said China was making significant gains in industries such as chemicals, machinery and industrial equipment, following earlier significant expansion of market share in industries such as electric vehicles and clean energy.
>“Global reliance on Chinese supply chains is deepening across a growing number of critical products,” Rhodium said, adding that China was using regulation and economic coercion to reinforce control over key supply chains. “The window for effective policy response is narrowing,” it added.
>Camille Boullenois, lead author of the report, told the FT that China’s evolving industrial policies posed a “real threat” to the economic engine of countries such as Germany and other advanced industrial economies.
>“China’s rise is broadly eroding some of the last areas where they still have a technological and industrial edge, like chemicals, autos, machinery and robotics,” she said. “China is gaining market share incredibly fast in these sectors. If countries don’t react now, the industrial landscape could look very different in just a few years.”
>The report noted that China’s most recent five-year plan had for the first time included a focus on advanced technologies such as biomanufacturing, nuclear fusion energy and brain-computer interfaces. This suggested that its industrial policy was evolving from focusing on strategic sectors to a “broader effort to reshape the entire industrial ecosystem”.
>The trade surplus growth represented success moving up the production value chain and exporting high-tech goods but also its success substituting domestic products for imports.

62238066
>>62238057
ai robots are coming. wake up and invest /biz/.

62238069
>>62238057
China is the new power and Usa is just coasting on past fumes. Only 10% of Americans are doing well and are carrying the economy and countru.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62238116
>>62238069
china is begging us for our chips lol. our tech companies are globally dominant.

62238199
>>62238116
No China isn’t. lol. China is aiming for self reliance. Also, there isn’t an industry china has entered and didn’t take over. >>62238116

62238205
>>62238069
> our tech companies are globally dominant
Not true. China took over the smartphone and ev industry. lol. Your thinking of China is very outdated.

62238208
>>62238116
> our tech companies are globally dominant
There isn’t a tech company usa has that China doesn’t have the equivalent of. lol.

62238216
>>62238116
> china is begging us for our chips lol
This arrogant american attitude is why Trump and the ultra wealthy have no idea how far behind usa is. I am sure musk and jensen know since they travel to china frequently. And ai and chips isnt a race.

62238241
>>62238216
It literally is a race and if you get far enough ahead nobody can ever catch you - hence NVDA stock.

62239362
>>62238241
China isnt far behind. And youre crazy to think Nvidia doesn’t want to sell to the Chinese market.
There isn’t any market Usa dominates.
There isn’t any market that China has entered and didn’t take over.
https://youtu.be/iB2eApp0Kmo

62239371
>>62238241
Then this means China always catches up and takes over. lol. Ai is the only thing usa seems to have a slight lead over the usa ACCORDING to msm. We, you and I, literally have no idea about this except what msm tells us. Listen to what even Jensen Huang says:
>China is not behind oops I meant China is not far behind.

62239377
>>62238199
>>62238216
china wants NVDAs latest chips.

>>62238208
thats obvious. no debate there. i said US tech is globally dominant as you can clearly re read if needed. they use Google in the EU not Baidu. They use Google in South America not Baidu. etc. not really complicated stuff. you can look up these global market shares if you want.

The world uses Amazon, MSFT, V, MA, Meta (instagram, whatsapp), etc. mcdonalds, coke, pep, boeing, on and on.

>>62239362
nvda cant sell their latest chips to china by US law.

>There isn’t any market Usa dominates.
google is dominant globally, meta, v, ma, etc

62239383
https://www.statista.com/statistics/1381664/worldwide-all-devices-market-share-of-search-engines/

62239392
https://companiesmarketcap.com/

first chinese company is Tencent at #25

62239394
>>62239377
China dominates ev and smart phones.
And youre talking about a search engine.
China wants to develop their own chips. Notice how China doesn’t allow their own companies to buy nvidia chips?
Nvidia is losing the chinese market and once china develops “good enough” or better chips, it will take over that.
Its inevitable. China takes over everything.
And EU wanted to replace eu with huawei 5g or whatever but usa intervened in 2018. It is 2026 and now the whole world knows china is a superpower and it is a multi polar world AT BEST for usa.

62239404
>>62239392
Market cap doesn’t matter and neither does stock market in a way for this topic. No one wants to put money in Chinese stocks for various reasons. You know what they are.

62239416
>>62239377
>instagram, whatsapp
China has tiktok and that other one that is popular for Chinese people. China has a billion plus people, simply being in popular in China is also enough in a way.
Usa went from #1 in everything 20+ years ago to sharing #1 in a few sectors as best with China.

62239428
>>62239377
Macdonalds is staffed by their own citizens in foreign countries while they are staffed by brown black immigrants and other types in usa.

item.title: Purple pill me on Kleros PNK — 19 replies — 43.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Purple pill me on Kleros PNK

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-05-16 17:41 UTC

OP

Purple pill me on Kleros PNK

Early replies (first 3)

62235289
>>62234810
I hate the French

62235307
zero

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62235386
>>62235289
Me too.

62235476
>>62234810
no idea what you just said but i like the picture

62237326
>>62234810
I fucking told you so, I dumped all my shit last year when they clearly showed massive incompetence.
I feel back for 25M bro who only woke up today, the slippage must have been rough.

>>62235289
Next time ask a french what good ever came out of a commie cooperative entity.

62237340
>>62237326
>spent 5 years on V2 supposed to scale courts with layer 2s, while L2s played out, them not delivering anything
Previous post of mine, I was wondering why I sold.
These fucks still haven't delivered V2 when the whole back to L1 debacle started.

Also the fact that I started my own business and now see clearly how unserious they are about scaling up.

62237361
>>62237340
No one cares about your life you dumb weirdo, but they are delivering v2 despite how slow they’ve been, it’s currently being audited. The mega whale who sold is pretty stupid. Nigga couldn’t wait until the end of summer jesus

62237370
>>62237361
kek, this board helped me become wealthy I try to give back
feel free to ignore me

62237374
>>62237370
you got burnt buying too high and you obsessively cry over your mistakes, seethe more no one should ever take advice from you

62237379
>>62237361
also...
>~50k
>megawhale
bruh I dropped that as a meme in venice, which pumped multiples.

>>62237374
yeah I'm giga salty about kleros because they wasted massive potential
even went to an IRL conf to feel the vibe of the team

62237392
>>62237379
nvm 350K, thought it was much more down in the shitter
anyway enjoy the hold

62237418
>>62237392
>gets burnt so bad buying too high follows kleros team around the world
>cant do basic math
>can barely speak English

kek

62237446
sirs... wtf happened?

62237529
>>62234810
I want to put my penis in her and make her bear my children if you know what I mean

62238260
>sirs... wtf happened?

62238308
>>62234810
BIG FAT ANIME TITTIEEEES YYUEEEAAAAAAAHHHHHHHHH BAAABBBYYYYYY

62239372
>>62237446
Clement’s dump

62239387
>>62234810
Brownpill me on getting a brown gf as a white man

item.title: I have tried to buy currency on probably 100 different occasions over the past 6 years and not once have I found a servi… — 5 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I have tried to buy currency on probably 100 different occasions over the past 6 years and not once have I found a servi…

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-05-16 18:06 UTC

OP

I have tried to buy currency on probably 100 different occasions over the past 6 years and not once have I found a service that does not demand my SSN and live selfie videos that will immediately enter the dark web (dark because it's Indians doing identity fraud) the moment the service goes down or KYC laws cuckold it into submission.

Early replies (first 3)

62239441
Crypto reached its logical end conclusion: desperate brown people trying to scam each other in the smoldering ruins of something white people built.

62239451
>>62239438
Jews already know everything about you, why not just do it? You're not protecting anything by avoiding it. If you really care that much there's probably a cryptobro in every town who would sell you some for dollars.

62239456
>>62239451
>just lose everything you have in 3 years when NiggerCumShot goes under and your KYC info mysteriously ends up in the hands of a human trafficker in Turkey

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62239457
what's the alternative? using p2p with a bizbro via metamask then wiring money to their bank?

62239460
>>62239441

item.title: Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main questio… — 60 replies — 42.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main questio…

Open thread • Replies: 60 • Updated: 2026-05-16 16:41 UTC

OP

Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main question is at the bottom below the dashed line, so you can skip all of this text if you want.

Is anyone here good with statistics or math? Idk the correct field that would apply here, some math shit.
I’ve been working on a strategy for years at this point, but I’ve been focused on a single thing for about the last half year. I’ve back tested it till 2020 and its results are the photo. I wanted to use it with prop firms to make some cash since the potential profit with them is way higher than using my own cash, but the drawdowns are too severe. And prop firms are hella strict on their drawdowns.
I’ve run the math and if I scale down enough to where I can trade without blowing up my account, at the rate of withdrawals and taxes and splits, I’d be working for nothing. Min wage basically. And if I scale up then I just blow up accounts due to drawdown. I don’t care about profit either. I need smoothness. Even if I take a major hit in points, so long as it becomes smoother that’s still fine since I can just scale up on prop firms.

It’s basic metrics right now are
WR: 53.78%
Profit Factor: 1.36
Trades Per Day: 8.33

There’s a lot more data but that’s the gist.
The strategy is a blend so it’s RR varies, but it’s pretty close to 1:1RR

—————————-
Here’s my main question: the strategy currently averages 8 trades a day. That’s a lot. Surely I can cut that down to 1-2 and increase the winrate right, therefore increasing its smoothness right? Surely. I’ve tried a lot of things but I’ve failed all analytical approaches. I’ve done a lot of digging and I think I’m just out of my element here.

I think my request is gonna fall on deaf ears but I’m hopeful. Maybe someone earnest and smarter than me can help.

Early replies (first 3)

62220033
BUmp before I sleep

62220056
buy an ad grok

62222767
>>62220056
Does my post sound like an Ai made it lol? It’s a Genuine question

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62228705
>>62228603
the strategy is not anything that would require low latency at least to my knowledge since the avg trade lasts anywhere from half an hour to several hours. again, i got no idea where to go from here. any tips would be appreciated

>>62228693
well if you helped me improve it, you could do whatever you want with it. It's not some proprietary strategy that requires some exotic data, it's pretty simple and generic. maybe that's why it's had a minor edge for so long. I would only be trading on it using prop firms, their sim accounts don't trade on live markets. and even if i did, i'm only trading NQ which is hella liquid. And as I've mentioned before, once I make a certain amount of money I'm done for good. Never gonna trade again. In theory if I could get this thing to a workable state, scaling it up would take a few months and I'd hit my goal within a year or two.

62228733
>>62224557
Depends on capital restrictions, an arbitrage or HFT strategy can easily get 1%/day but probably has limits on how much capital it can grow into.

62228952
>>62228733
lol

62228956
add 5% annual IBKR fees itll auto divide for margin or funding then 10 ticks of slippage

62229415
I still have yet to see someone who can help.

>>62228733
1% a day seems impossible in the long run. No strategy can have only good days with no bad days.

>>62228956
If they are buying and selling contracts to such an extent then fees would def eat into it. But if it’s a single buy or sell, or a position that’s held for hours then I don’t see why not.

62229502
Bump

62230495
>>62229415
I coded up orb it doesnt beat fees the original paper is a meme in the algorithmic trading community

try on TQQQ

62230578
>>62230495
if you want to talk serious share discord username

62230627
Based OP, a genuinely original effort thread with potential. Will continue to lurk for interest and hopes to obtain some knowledge, but this is out of scope

62232509
>>62230495
It had basis on ORB and VWAP but isn't just that.

>>62230578
Before any of that I'd like to hear your ideas on what you'd do / suggest for me to improve the strategy. I've already tried a lot of things, so I'd like to know how you could help me.

>>62230627
Thanks, but I haven't really gotten anything good out of this thread yet hahaha

62232797
bump

>>62230627
Also I thought about this a bit more, edge erosion is a real thing. The ironic thing is that if I released it with the intention of helping others, some actual firm might come along and pick that up and run with it. I recall there was this one strategy I was researching a while back. It performed well from 2016 all the way until 2020. But after 2020 till now it started performing worse and worse and worse. Until 2025 and 2026 where the returns were so ass that the only way you'd profit off of it was to essentially scalp it down to the ticks. Only a group with low latency and a huge bankroll would profit off of that. I saw live how a strategy had it's edge eroded.

I'm not claiming what I have is something that amazing. but that is a worry. At the most I'd share it with close friends and family and help them succeed. After all, what is the point of wealth if not to help others.

62233623
Bump

62234201
Bumpinonce more before I sleep

62236353
bump once more. is there one out here who's got the smarts?

62237934
>>62236353
all im going to say is your approach is too simple i thought orb had alpha decay years ago lol

62238015
>>62237934
Anon, I'd like some constructive feedback. Regardless of whether the approach is simple or not, the fact of the matter is that the data backs it up. Only one of the lines uses ORB.

The full blend, as shown in the first picture, is split across 5 different lines. The correlation between them, while not perfectly non-overlapping, is still low enough that it’s rare for them all to lose on the same day.

In the days since I made this, I was actually able to find a way to further cut down a few outliers, reducing total trades taken by 16% while increasing points by about 9%.

But these are just marginal improvements, and I’m hesitant to keep chasing them due to fears of overfitting.

My hypothesis is that, with how many trades I take, I should be able to quantitatively chop down the worst of them. I’ve already managed to do that with relatively simple filters over the past few days, but I’m running up against a wall, so I want to avoid going too far. THAT's why I made this thread. In hopes of finding someone with knowledge.

62238067
>>62238015
ok, now we're getting somewhere. you are afraid of overfitting and not even using ML lmfao noob

62238083
>>62238067
Rather than implement something I have no understanding of, I'm sticking to what I do know. I'll say it again. if you wanna offer criticism or advice, keep it constructive. just saying "ML" is so vague. Be more specific. ML in what way?

62238370
>>62238083
any sort of model.predict() is going to overfit unless you train on like 20-30% of the data thats implicitly going to be trained or predicted on, so with pure ta or price action strategies overfitting is never possible since theyre raw calculations of mathematics on price action time series data


holy shit anon you should know this im sharing liquid gold intuition with you

62238387
>>62219625
That equity curve is either a data issue or clearly just picking pennies up infront of a steamroller.
You being obssessed with "smoothness" is also a red flag.
You thinking "the data backs it up" with confidence is also a BIG red flag, this is not how real trading works.
Anyone can just run some lookahead biased regressions and uncover a "strategy", anything that is actually statistically robust and ACTIONABLE will be based off of forward walks and have effect sizes so small you'll be questioning whether its even real, and only THEN have you found something that might actually indeed be real.

62238401
>>62238370
I tried a variety of strategies based on price action but I could never get any of them to work. This setup is the best I could get. I tried other things but they wouldn’t work. I’m still not understanding how this ML would filter out losses in a way that’s actionable. If it just gives me some highly specific bucket like “trades between 0.75% and 0.84% of OR width are losing” then that’s just over fitting.

>>62238387
I’ve mentioned why. It’s for the sake of prop firms. They have hard drawdown rules. As mentioned before, i can theoretically pass and even get on a funded account with the strat as it is now. But it takes too long to pass and too long to get a payout. Most of what I read in ur response sounds like fluff no offense. I’m all for constructive criticism but I don’t see how any of what you said applies to my.

My strategy is a blend of 5 simple ideas that each trade on their own with the idea of:

Rather than find one single strategy that is good and trades often,
Find multiple good strategies that don’t trade often individually and then use them together.

I haven’t done any look back biased regressions, I have a separate 2026 only sample that I’ll test my strategies on to make sure they’re not overfit. And they’ll work just fine on new never before seen data.

Guys I am open to any constructive criticism, just please make it make sense in the context of what I’m asking.

62238500
>>62238401
Listen bro you don't just throw a couple of funny numbers in a computer and cash comes out thats just not how the world works.
You believe you need to be super opaque so we don't "steal" your strategy, but there are thousands of guys like you who think they have some top secret "strat" that they just need someone to fund them for.
What model are you even using? Some GARCH? HMM? Frequency of your data?

62238660
> top secret strategy trades the Nasdaq, an index that only goes up
> it makes money
> anon thinks he's a genius
> loses to buy-and-hold TQQQ

62238773
>>62238500
I’m not being opaque. I said I want advice from someone who has knowledge in fields related to statistics and quantitative analysis. Do you?

No, I don’t use GARCH. My strategy does not tune itself, it does not use some regressive or adjusting method. It follows the same rules trading in 2021 as it does in 2025 in the data set. The TP and SL’s are the only dynamic variable because they are based on VWAP, or ORB, or the volume profile as well as fixed % based on the open market value. A 20 point move in 2020 is far different than a 20 point move in 2025.
But they are not hand tuned or adjusted by me. It sets the TP based on the given range. It’s as simple as that.

I only used volume and OHLC of NQ and ES via 1m candles.

Also who the hell said I need funding? You should read all of my messages before making assumptions.

>>62238660
The worst drawdowns were approximately 3,000 points, while peak equity was around 50,000 points.

This is just napkin math, but let’s make a few simplifying assumptions:

Assume 1 NQ contract per trade. Some lines had multiple take-profits, but we’ll simplify. NQ is $20 per point, so a 3,000-point drawdown would require $60,000 in capital to withstand the worst observed drawdown. To be more conservative, let’s round that up to $100,000 of starting cash. Unexessary but let’s do it anyways.

Starting with $100,000, if running equity reaches 50,000 points, that’s 50,000 × $20 = $1,000,000.

So, theoretically, starting with $100,000 and ending around $1 million is possible under these assumptions.

TQQQ went up about 230% from 2020 till 2025, where my data ends.

230% vs 900%.

If I had the money, I’d just put it all into SCHD and chill. But I don’t. That’s why I made this thread. That’s why I’m making this strategy and asking if anyone here can offer ideas that make sense in the context of what I’m asking.

62238820
>>62238773
duh you don't hold TQQQ through 2022 retard
I already understand you don't want to apply any brains but jesus

62239204
>>62238500
lmfao, garch, mm, and HMM are all useless in trading

62239213
>>62238773
try this just for the lols and its like throwing shit to a wall hoping it sticks

code the strategy using an external data api use every talib indicator an oscilator then denoise the indicators then shove that as a filter and vectorize the backtest extract the highest sharpe ratio or net return in similar sharpe give or take in your datamining script

62239220
>>62239213
if you dont know anything about genetic algorithms watch coding trains NEAT flappy bird series its quite useful information and intuition for genetic algorithms doesnt have to be neuroevolution since these are just indicators you wont be topologically ranking these to another sorting neuron layer you just need to mutate and generate fitness effectively

62239225
if all of this is above your head just fucking quit quant isnt cut out for noobs its cut out for mathematics genius' and physicists

62239227
also i gave your ass free alpha i expect you to post source code now

item.title: You should be holding at least 1 BTC. — 93 replies — 41.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
You should be holding at least 1 BTC.

Open thread • Replies: 93 • Updated: 2026-05-16 15:52 UTC

OP

Imagine not owning at least 1 BTC before institutions and governments vacuum the remaining supply while you’re still debating if Bitcoin is a scam… or just trading crypto without considering BTC long term.

Early replies (first 3)

62219945
>>62219942
Or I can trade alts (leveraged BTC) and actually make it instead of getting a 2x pump

62219995
Fuck. I have 2BTC . Is that enough bro?

62220027
>>62219995
no, i have 3. and it's not enough, we're just poor faggots

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62231072
>>62229557
Why though anon? It’s not like this is some random vaporware project. They already have partnerships with names like GoMining, Ledger, a16zcrypto and others around the ecosystem.

62231081
>>62230004
Why all in? : (

62231090
>>62229433
Good advice so far.

62232211
>>62222594
There's solution for this

62233500
>>62231072
is that legit?

62233508
>>62231066
You hate woman because you like man. Gayish bitcoiner

62233709
>>62233500
Yeah anon. There’s already almost 51k BTC staked in the protocol too, which is like $4B+ worth of BTC already participating around the ecosystem.

62233716
>>62223021
You can always keep slowly adding over time anon. Most people don’t even have close to 1 BTC.

62233736
>>62224655
it will be your opportunity as well.

62233806
>>62229570
I'm from Spain and I live well. Got small biz + doing crypto + forex + stocks. Life is good.

62233821
>>62233709
never though there's protocol exists like this

62233827
>buy btc
buy link
buy ICP
buy avax

62233835
>>62228833
And Ethereum is the godess

62233840
>>62228631
Hes just a homosexual and fears for the virginity of his anus

62233880
>>62233827

worst trash available
>UNGMI

62233986
>>62233821
Yeah anon, it already exists but I think a lot of people just still aren’t paying attention to the newer BTCFi infra yet. And it’s still pretty early too since the TBVs haven’t even launched yet, which should unlock even more native BTC use cases.

62233993
>>62225388
See this anon?

62233997
>>62224679
The funniest part is you probably think debasing fiat printed out of thin air is somehow more “real” than a globally traded asset with trillions in liquidity.

62234000
>>62230021
Gonna be dead broke without BTC lol.

62234021
>>62233806
Aren't you fucked with taxes and DAC8 now, I cannot buy anymore via a cex here

62234099
>>62233840
It sucks being gay

62234109
>>62234021
do the alternative. Privacy

62234248
>>62234021
Government is shit.

62234899
>>62233986
I don’t think that’s enough when ETH literally lives and dies by DeFi activity.

62235825
>>62222594
you don't need to

62237090
>>62220146
give me money

62237423
>>62219995
you're rich mate.

62238430
>>62229077
Exactly, but turning BTC into an active asset will through native staking, instead of just worrying about future risks.

62238954
>>62234248
I'm not surprised lol

62239105
>just buy into the obvious ponzi scheme bro

item.title: I'm 33 and still working an entry level job as a BDR in tech/SaaS. — 21 replies — 13.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
I'm 33 and still working an entry level job as a BDR in tech/SaaS.

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-05-16 16:36 UTC

OP

I'm 33 and still working an entry level job as a BDR in tech/SaaS. What the fuck do i do now? I don't even like what I do

Early replies (first 3)

62236318
>enter thread
>download op image
>leave thread

62236322
>>62236309
Sex!

62236326
>>62236309
I deserve to have sex with women who look like this

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62236330
>>62236309
At least you’re not a 30 year old entry level analyst dealing with retarded ass sales reps all day long. I bet you get paid better too.

62236409
>>62236309
That is a job for someone literally a decade younger than you. Why not just get an AE job?

62236420
>>62236409
I might have at this point. My current employer is Gate keeping me from getting an AE promotion. I've applied so many times for remote AE positions , but they won't hire you unless you have previous AE experience. I might have to start applying to on site roles now which will suck, but at least if they accept me then I'll have some experience

62236517
>>62236309
I'm 33 and work in Walmart as a stocker

62236592
>>62236420
It's cuz you're (literally) competing against 500-1000+ applicants per role, even including the on-site roles. And no, baseddit strategies like
> just go to the last page on linkedin bro, less applicants to compete against that way bro
> you can't give up bro! it's a numbers game bro!
will not work because everyone else out there is already using the same strategy, hence the flood of applicants per job which make it impossible to even get an interview. And for that linkedin point, used to work but not anymore because every job posted on there has the stupid "promoted" tag which exposes it to the entire globe of applicants within seconds. Each job posting gets bot applicant flooded within micro seconds. Many of these applicants also have rockstar-tier resumes too so good luck competing against them.

tldr; job market is PERMANENTLY fucked, thank AI and globalism for this mess

62236645
>>62236309
>33
>entry level
nigga you lost a whole decade of your life you need to figure out what happened there

62236684
>>62236318
kek

62236700
>>62236517
How long have you been working there? Was it easy to get the job?

62236733
>>62236517
Become a stonker.

62237056
>>62236309
just get promoted to AE like the rest of the sales monkeys. its not any harder. literally apply yourself to applying for better roles the way you prospect for opportunities in your day job u sped

62237118
>>62236318
literally me

62237179
>>62237118
It's some kind of long format ai clip I found but didn't save in time
Shit is hot af

62237198
>>62237179
there you go

62237427
>>62237198
This AI shit is to gooners what crack cocaine was to black neighbourhoods in the 80s. We are not far off from everyone just being able to generate their 10/10 goon material at the press of a button and edge 24/7. It's Joever (at least for your prostates)

62237463
>>62237198
>>62237427
Coomer here. Maybe I'm just old, but AI shit kills my boner the moment I see it. I can't get over the uncanny valley feeling.

62237611
>>62237198
Do you have that one where a goth egirl poses with a boomer, then the scene switches to them both covered in sweat like they both had rough sex?

62237614
>>62237427
24/7 gooning sounds bullish for cloud providers and electricity generation.

62239211
>>62237463
Same it's just not there yet.

>>62237557

62237705
Thanks for playing bro

62238085
>>62237557
Do you know why?? that's so fucked

62238110
>>62237557
>requesting the PC monitors they sent back
Ghost them. What are they going to do? Not hire you harder.

62238129
>>62238085
Non violent felony from 6 years ago.
Pearl clutching fags will deny a j*b to someone with the degree and experience but won't bat an eye at a felon running the country.

62238145
>>62238129
Start your own business. No one wants to take the liability

62238182
>>62237557
Learn a trade that is in demand no one will care about your past faggot.

62238197
>>62237557
I’m so sorry dude. Felonies are a tough cross to bear.

62238204
>>62238182
This. My buddy was a skinhead tweaker convict. Then he met Jesus and learned to weld. Now he is doing good.

62238213
>>62238182
Too late
Everyone else learned the trades and now there's an oversupply

62238235
ive got a drug possession felony, i feel you
i have no idea what to do job wise so im probably going to end up having to own my own business if I ever plan on making it

or just kms who knows

62238245
I just left the USA and went to Europe and now I work at a bank even tho I am massively behind on debts and child support in the USA.

62238261
>>62237557
Tell them you'll put them out on the street and they can pick them up when they like.

62239021
>>62238213
This is not true.

62239031
>>62238129
That's fucked sorry anon.

62239042
>>62237557
if you "send them back" with items similar in weight to the monitors but the box looks tampered with then you could feasibly say that you sent them back but they were stolen. not that i would recommend doing that at all; please don't do that.

62239048
>>62238129
What a perfect prerequisite to be a plumber.

62239063
>>62237557
that you, timmy? dafuq you gawn do?

62239092
>>62239042

62239128
>>62238129
Had a former friend steal my credit card info and charge about 500 dollars worth of purchases to it. Pretty sure he got hit with a couple felonies. I hope it comes back to bite him in the ass like this. God knows I fucking hate him. I hope I ruined his life.

62239177
>>62237617

item.title: Kraken has moved to CCIP. — 59 replies — 8.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Kraken has moved to CCIP.

Open thread • Replies: 59 • Updated: 2026-05-16 11:22 UTC

OP

kek fuddies

Early replies (first 3)

62230910
>>62230891
Time to dump btc from 81 and eth from 2300

62230914
WAGMI WAGMI

62230921
>shovel salesman announces that shovels are useful, actually

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62231853
>>62230891
>>62231282

Lido too now lol
Fresh off the presses

62231868
>>62231843
It was foretold. Obvious in hindsight.

62231901
cuckolds are embarassing lmao

price + chart

62231946
>>62231853
ALL AT ONCE

62231990
>>62231853
oooooooof

62231992
>>62231946
OLD NEWS buddy

62232282
>>62230891

This is actually FUD. Cross Chain Interoperability Protocol is a load of gobbly gook (I skimmed thoroughly) and chainlink still hasn't proven its original use case (solving the problem of getting data into smart contracts in a decentralized manner).

The very concept of Sergey's oracle system is unproven, although sensible. All this bullshit being put on top is not "exciting" or "value-added," it is FUD. It is a stream of bullshit without any actual real world uses.

You retards are getting excited by someone scooping shit on top of technological marvels. Imagine the first airplane, thats a blockchain. Now imagine a woman jumping on top the cockpit and taking a huge dump. Thats what your getting excited about.

62232408
>>62231646
Saar I'm sooo white saar!

62232418
Wait I thought LINK was a SCAM?!? You're telling me the most upstanding, honest, and forthcoming company in crypto, King Kraken, has literally entrusted their reputation in the hands of a fat russian big mac enjoyer? So LINK is not a scam? Somebody comfort me please...

62232437
>>62232418
the scoundrel sergey is very convincing

62232441
>>62230891
Thats cool and all but.... one small problem.... uhh... price??? Oh...... and... chart??

62232461
>>62232408
that's adem newfaggot
lurk moar

62232525
>>62232282
This so much, all the top financial institutions of the world are getting absolutely gobblecocked by sergey

Now X R P. THAT is the one correct thing they are choosing!!

62236073
>>62231115
Hello mentally ill faggot how are you doing today?

62236163
>>62231853
This is old news isn't it? Lido was already with CCIP. They're just now publishing their rationale behind the decision.

62236196
>>62236073
doing great thanks
oh btw: pee plus see, bitch

62236449
>>62231646

>>62231646

dude that's fucking wild

have you told anyone irl?

62236468
>>62236449

62236472

>CCIP fees last 24h: $637

bahahahaha

>Link price action last 24h: -5.2%

bahahahahahahahaah holy shit


nobody, and i mean NOBODY, gets viciously c u c k h o l d e d quite like the boyscouts.... and then comes back time and time again, absolutely BEGGING for more

62236479
>>62230910
Oh my fuckk you weren't even lying.

I hate this gay ass market.

62236498
>>62231117
You know he probably owns more Link than almost anyone else here, hes just be a dickhole for fun because he knows whats coming.

62236508
>>62230891
i saw kraken now allows margin gambling without having to have a huge portfolio. pretty cool imo. as far as link, i don't think about it at all

62236594
>>62236163
Well here's a completely new one.

62236605
OMFG WE ARE MOONING!!!!!

62236616
HOLY FUCKING SHIT IT JUST KEEPS GOING!!!!!

62236626
>faggot ass stinkies gloating over nothing
This thread is now the $9 dollar waiting room

62236630
can't wait until the failure of stink is so obvious, even the delulu marines cannot deny it any longer

they will be forced to admit that poverty is their only bedfellow

62237484
>>62236630
the thing is this grift can go on for about 8 more years, the next bull will take link up to 17 and then 4 years until it bottoms to 2.50-5 dollars, then next bull will take link to maybe 8-9 dollars. so its going to be about 12 years until the marines realize they fucked up.


by then people like chainlink god will just point out hurdles that chainlik has had.

chainlink reserves will be dumped and i pray to god that another competitor comes and takes away some of the defi market share chainlink has got.

62238651
>>62237484

62238716
>>62231853

62237684
>>62237477

62238918
>>62237477

62238922

62239273
>>62237519

item.title: Crypto is no more — 11 replies — 6.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Crypto is no more

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-05-16 16:28 UTC

OP

AI stole the hype from crypto and by EOY we're going to witness the greatest crash ever.
Of course, it will be a good time to buy then, but for now stay out of this market.

Early replies (first 3)

62234527
>>62234481
proof??

62234532
>>62234527
He knows the future and thats why hes a trillionaire

62234653
>>62234532
omg

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62234709
>>62234481
AI will inevitably integrate with distributed networks. Cryptocurrencies like Doge and Litecoin may become worthless, but ones tied to computation and storage are going to the moon.

62235311
>>62234709
No they won't.

62235544
>>62235311
If I want to buy ice cream, and store A sells it for $5 and store B sells it for $1, which store will I buy from?

62235568
$30 trillion is sitting on the sidelines waiting to get 3.8% stablecoin yield on coinbase pro

62236015
>>62234481

62236144
Crypto is dead, OP is right. Everything rotated into AI, then the meltdown will happen and obviously drag crypto with it. $50k btc eoy heil hitler

62239016
>>62234481
no point buying idiot it is never going back up again. Future is AI only - nothing for crypto

62239183
